project.title: Candida albicans whole cell proteomics
project.projectDescription: Proteomics analysis of Candida albicans 90028 whole cells.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Three independently cell cultures were submitted to metabolite, protein and lipid extraction (MPLEx) according to the protocol by Nakayasu et al. (DOI: 10.1128/mSystems.00043-16). Extracted proteins were digested with trypsin and resulting peptides were extracted with 1 mL Discovery C18 SPE columns (Supelco, Bellefonte, PA) as previously described (DOI: 10.1128/mSphere.00085-15). Digested peptides were suspended in water, quantified by BCA assay and 0.5 μg of peptides were loaded into trap column (4 cm x 100 μm ID packed in-house with 5 μm C18, Jupiter). Peptide separation was carried out an analytical column (70 cm x 75 µm ID packed with C18, 3 μm particles) using a gradient of acetonitrile/0.1% formic acid (solvent B) in water/0.1% formic acid (solvent A). The flow was set to 300 nL/min with 1% solvent B and kept for 15 min. Then concentration of solvent B was increased linearly as following: 19 min, 8% B; 60 min, 12% B; 155 min, 35% B; 203 min, 60% B; 210 min, 75% B; 215 min, 95% B; 220 min, 95% B.  Eluting peptides were directly analyzed by electrospray in an orbitrap mass spectrometer (Q-Exactive Plus, Thermo Fisher Scientific) by scanning a window of 400-2000 m/z with resolution of 70,000 at m/z 400. Tandem mass spectra were collected using HCD (32% NCE) on the 12 most intense multiple-charged parent ions at a resolution of 17,500.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Mass spectrometry data was analyzed using MaxQuant software (v.1.5.5.1) (doi: 10.1038/nprot.2016.136). Peptide identification was performed by searching against the C. albicans SC5314 sequences from Uniprot Knowledge Base (downloaded December 6, 2017). Searching parameters included the variable modifications protein N-terminal acetylation and oxidation of methionine, in addition to carbamidomethylation of cysteine residues. Parent and fragment mass tolerance were kept as the default setting of the software. Only fully tryptic digested peptides were considered, allowing up to two missed cleaved sites per peptide.

references.referenceLine: Zamith-Miranda D, Heyman HM, Cleare LG, Couvillion SP, Clair GC, Bredeweg EL, Gacser A, Nimrichter L, Nakayasu ES, Nosanchuk JD. Multi-omics Signature of Candida auris, an Emerging and Multidrug-Resistant Pathogen. mSystems. 2019 4(4)

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: Three independent cell cultures were submitted to metabolite, protein, and lipid extraction (MPLEx) according to the protocol by Nakayasu et al..
Characteristics[Specimen]: cell pellets were frozen until the protein, metabolite, and lipid extractions.
Comment[Instrument]: orbitrap mass spectrometer (Q-Exactive Plus; Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer]: orbitrap mass spectrometer (Q-Exactive Plus; Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[IonizationType]: Eluting peptides were directly analyzed by electrospray in an orbitrap mass spectrometer (Q-Exactive Plus; Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: Tandem mass spectra were collected using high-energy collision dissociation (32% normalized collision energy) on the 12 most intense multiple-charged parent ions at a resolution of 17,500.
Comment[Separation]: trap column (4 cm by 100 μm inner diameter [ID], packed in-house with 5 μm C18; Jupiter)
analytical column (70 cm x 75 μm ID packed with C18, 3-μm particles)
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: allowing up to two missed cleaved sites per peptide.
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: Parent and fragment mass tolerance were kept as the default setting of the software.
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: Parent and fragment mass tolerance were kept as the default setting of the software.
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: scanning a window of 400 to 2,000 m/z with a resolution of 70,000 at m/z 400
